changesProposed.name: 1X Technologies

verified.name: 1X Technologies
verified.description: Norwegian-American robotics and artificial intelligence company developing general-purpose humanoid robots for home environments. The company manufactures bipedal humanoid robots including the NEO model designed for private home use.
verified.notes: Company rebranded from Halodi Robotics to 1X Technologies in 2022. Wikipedia sources refer to the robotics company. Sources [0] and [1] describe a different 1X Technologies LLC (a wire and cable distributor founded 2015 in Sheridan, Wyoming) - these are distinct companies and sources [0] and [1] are not used for verification. Total raised calculated from Series A2 ($23.5M in March 2023) + Series B ($100M in January 2024) = $123.5M.

changesProposed.name: Applied Intuition

verified.name: Applied Intuition
verified.description: Software platform for physical AI that powers autonomous vehicles, industrial machines, and defense systems. The company provides simulation tools and an autonomy stack used by major OEMs and defense contractors.
verified.notes: Founded January 2017. Series F funding of $600M announced June 2025, co-led by BlackRock-managed funds and Kleiner Perkins, raising valuation to $15B from previous $6B in 2024. Employs approximately 1,300 people as of 2025.

verified.name: Asimov
verified.description: Provides an integrated platform for genetic design including engineered host cells, genetic parts libraries, and cloud-based design software for advanced therapeutic manufacturing applications.

changesProposed.name: Boom Supersonic

current.name: Boom Supersonic
current.founder: Blake Scholl, Joe Wilding, Josh Krall

verified.name: Boom Supersonic
verified.description: American aerospace company developing the Overture, a proposed supersonic airliner designed to cruise at Mach 1.7. The company has built and flight-tested the XB-1 demonstrator, which broke the sound barrier on January 28, 2025.
verified.notes: XB-1 became first privately funded aircraft to break the sound barrier on January 28, 2025, reaching Mach 1.122. Company has 130 orders/pre-orders from United Airlines, American Airlines, and Japan Airlines for Overture. Headquarters listed as Centennial, Colorado in Wikipedia; company founded in Denver, Colorado in 2014.

verified.name: Cerebras
